home.contact.body: Always interested in conversations about data engineering, automation, applied AI, multi-agent systems, knowledge engineering, agent infrastructure, especially where they meet regulated workflows.

home.cv.skills.heading: Skills
home.hero.lead: I'm Tom. I build AI-augmented automation for the operational work in regulated finance: regulatory notes, accounts quality review, document workflows, mailbox triage, and the reusable tooling that ties them together. Six years in Luxembourg fund services across PE, Private Debt and Real Estate, now spent designing the systems that do the work instead of doing it by hand. This site is the evidence: a live CV, the patterns behind the work, and case studies of it shipping.
home.hero.meta.casestudies.label: case studies on file
home.hero.meta.languages.label: languages spoken
home.hero.meta.years.label: in Lux fund services
home.languages.heading: Six, used regularly.
home.now.blurb.post: . I update it when things change, not on a schedule.
home.now.blurb.pre: A public note of where my head is right now, in the spirit of
home.now.heading: What I'm focused on this quarter.
home.now.items.compute.v: Cloud computing and GPU inference, to serve models faster and at lower marginal cost.
home.now.items.email.v: End-to-end email automation as a personal architecture. An incoming message triggers an agent that fetches attachments, reads them, searches across knowledge systems, delegates research, and once the answer is well grounded drafts the reply and proposes which attachments to include. A self-review loop runs the draft through a second model until it has no further comments, then hands a clean output to a human reviewer who owns the decision.
home.now.items.exocortex.v: An agentic memory system on my own infrastructure. A voice-first assistant over AR glasses with a memory that consolidates during sleep, running across two machines that talk to each other, turning ideas into video journals or music within hours.
home.now.items.frontier.v: Frontier models on large, long-context files. How far the current generation holds up running real workflows over very large inputs.
home.now.items.orchestration.v: Workflow orchestration with n8n, extending the automation surface beyond a single agent.
home.now.items.research.v: Applied research on frontier models and frontier technologies. Curiosity is the throughline across all of it.
home.reading.blurb: Themes I keep coming back to. A proper bookshelf with titles will go here once I've trimmed the list.
home.reading.heading: Mostly non-fiction.
home.reading.themes.0.n: How operational excellence actually gets built and kept. Applies to finance ops as much as to engineering.
home.reading.themes.1.n: Encoding expertise into tools other people can use without becoming experts themselves.
home.reading.themes.2.n: Practical books on what current AI can and cannot do, and where it changes the work.
home.reading.themes.3.n: Continuing the line my degrees started. International economics, capital markets, regulation.
home.work.blurb: Sanitised. Employer name, entity names and figures removed. Notes on how I think about this kind of work in fund services.
home.work.case.01.blurb: A pattern for how a Claude skill can read a trial balance, identify entity type, and generate disclosure notes in a firm's chosen style. Tables, movement schedules, narrative.
home.work.case.01.label: order of magnitude time savings on this pattern
home.work.case.01.title: Automated Regulatory Notes Generation
home.work.case.02.blurb: How I think about pre-audit cross-checking. A pattern that takes annual accounts (PDF) and source reconciliation (Excel), checks every cross-reference between primary statements and notes, and flags inconsistencies before the auditor finds them.
home.work.case.02.label: automated cross-checker
home.work.case.02.title: Annual Accounts Quality Review
home.work.case.03.blurb: A pattern for aggregating raw transactional share-register data into net positions per shareholder per share class, applying rule-based redaction for non-strategic shareholders, and producing print-ready Excel and PDF.
home.work.case.03.label: share registers for KYC/AML reviews
home.work.case.03.title: Confidential Document Anonymisation
home.work.case.04.blurb: A pattern for triaging email and meeting-transcript backlogs into prioritised morning briefings. Surfaces unanswered messages, follow-ups, and half-finished commitments, organised by priority and workstream.
home.work.case.04.label: morning briefing automation
home.work.case.04.title: Cross-Platform Mailbox Intelligence
home.work.case.05.blurb: Patterns for documenting payment and accounting workflows in a way new joiners can self-serve. Payment flow, exception handling, approval routing, recharge and intercompany cases.
home.work.case.05.label: structured payment and accounting guide
home.work.case.05.title: Process Documentation & Knowledge Transfer
home.work.case.06.blurb: Patterns I've worked through during fund-accounting platform rollouts. Pref-share reconciliation logic, waterfall validation approaches, edge-case discovery.
home.work.case.06.label: contribution to platform rollout
home.work.case.06.title: Fund Accounting Platform Integration
home.work.case.07.blurb: A library of small, opinionated AI skill patterns. Notes generation, quality review, anonymisation, mailbox triage, classification. Each with deterministic inputs and outputs. From single-purpose skills to five-persona orchestration, see Projects.
home.work.case.07.label: of reusable Claude skill patterns
home.work.case.07.title: AI Skill Architecture & Reusable Tooling
home.work.heading: Patterns I've worked through.
home.workflow.blurb: Point AI at the manual workflows underneath a finance desk. It surfaces what is unbooked, mismatched, or missing, builds the upload template, and drafts the counterparty email. The operator reviews and signs off; the human owns the decision. Click through a before-and-after process map and run it live.
home.workflow.heading: See the automation run, on synthetic data.
home.workflow.stage.build.desc: Generates the upload template for the accounting system and drafts the counterparty email.
home.workflow.stage.build.label: Build
home.workflow.stage.find.desc: Reads the source files and surfaces what is unbooked, mismatched, or missing.
home.workflow.stage.find.label: Find
home.workflow.stage.scale.desc: The same approach maps every other workflow in the company, from the existing mailbox.
home.workflow.stage.scale.label: Scale
home.workflow.stage.signoff.desc: The operator reviews and approves. The human owns the decision.
home.workflow.stage.signoff.label: Sign off

os.hint: Drag a window by its title bar. Double-click the bar to maximize.
projects.index.card.devswarm.body: A FastMCP server that orchestrates five persistent Claude Code persona sessions (architect, researcher, frontend, backend, reviewer-deployer) to turn one idea into a working pull request. Fire-and-forget; no Docker, no daemon, no port. Just files on disk and stdio MCP.
projects.index.card.devswarm.label: from one prompt to one PR, end-to-end
projects.index.card.devswarm.title: DevSwarm v1
projects.index.card.exocortex.body: A personal AI assistant accessible through Even Realities G2 smart glasses. Voice from the lenses, pipeline through self-hosted infrastructure on Fedora, all routed through Claude Max OAuth so there's no per-token API billing. Multilingual: English, German, Russian, French.
projects.index.card.exocortex.label: day 5 · base process complete
projects.index.card.exocortex.title: Personal AI Exocortex
projects.index.sub: Small experiments in agent design, knowledge tooling, and the workflow plumbing between them. Built for myself first, written up here when something works. Each card opens a longer write-up: architecture, decisions, and the artifacts the build produced.
thesis.eyebrow: MSc Thesis · 2020 · Erasmus School of Economics
thesis.meta.author: Author
thesis.s1.h1.body: EU members' exports are higher with each other than with non-EU partners.
thesis.s1.h1.label: EU membership.
thesis.s1.h2.body: Regional Trade Agreements increase aggregate exports between members of a common RTA.
thesis.s1.h2.label: RTAs.
thesis.s1.h3.body: A shared currency expands trade through reduced transaction fees and the absence of FX risk.
thesis.s1.h3.label: Common currency.
thesis.s1.h4.body: Improvements in the exporter's institutional quality promote bilateral aggregate exports.
thesis.s1.h4.label: Institutional quality.
thesis.s1.h5.body: Better exporter infrastructure boosts trade through efficiency gains and lower implicit trade costs.
thesis.s1.h5.label: Infrastructure.
thesis.s1.h6.body: Higher exporter globalization boosts trade via interdependence between elements of the global economy.
thesis.s1.h6.label: Globalization.
thesis.s1.h7.body: An increase in most-favored-nation tariffs negatively impacts bilateral trade.
thesis.s1.h7.label: Tariffs.
thesis.s1.lede: The paper tests seven hypotheses against the OECD panel: three on standard trade-policy variables (EU, RTAs, common currency), four extending the gravity model into institutional quality, infrastructure, globalization, and tariff barriers.
thesis.s2.find.eu.body: Both reporter and partner being EU or RTA members raises bilateral exports; common currency raises them further still.
thesis.s2.find.eu.title: EU, RTAs, common currency
thesis.s2.find.fragility.body: Higher state fragility on the exporter side meaningfully depresses bilateral exports; consistent with the institutions-as-trade-cost view.
thesis.s2.find.fragility.title: State fragility
thesis.s2.find.glob.body: Both globalization indices contribute substantially and consistently to bilateral exports across the full and recent (2011-2018) periods. The paper's headline contribution.
thesis.s2.find.glob.title: Economic & political globalization
thesis.s2.find.innovation.body: An exporter's innovation capacity has a significant, positive impact on bilateral aggregate exports.
thesis.s2.find.innovation.title: Innovation index
thesis.s2.find.roads.body: Among infrastructure indicators, road quality stands out with a substantial positive effect on the dependent variable.
thesis.s2.find.roads.title: Road infrastructure quality
thesis.s2.find.stability.body: Both come in negative; the most perplexing results. Possible explanation: stable, open exporters service larger domestic markets and source more inputs locally, dampening measured export volume.
thesis.s2.find.stability.title: Political stability & market openness
thesis.s2.lede: Estimated with fixed-effects OLS over 1995-2018, with all specifications passing the RESET test for misspecification. The results split cleanly into three buckets: robust positives, expected negatives, and the perplexing.
thesis.s3.contrib.c1.body: First gravity-model treatment of the KOF economic and political globalization indices on OECD exports; both come out significant and positive.
thesis.s3.contrib.c1.label: Globalization, end-to-end.
thesis.s3.contrib.c2.body: Brings a fragility dimension to a literature that traditionally proxies institutions with WGI / Heritage indicators alone.
thesis.s3.contrib.c2.label: State fragility added.
thesis.s3.contrib.c3.body: Tests the Global Innovation Index directly against bilateral exports; significant, positive, and robust.
thesis.s3.contrib.c3.label: Innovation as a determinant.
thesis.s3.contrib.c4.body: Re-runs the full model on the post-crisis sub-period 2011-2018 to surface effects that wash out over longer panels.
thesis.s3.contrib.c4.label: Two windows, not one.
thesis.s3.lede: Most gravity-model papers stop at GDP, distance, RTAs, and currency. This thesis extends the equation with three under-tested exporter-side variables and re-runs everything on a shorter, more recent sub-period.
thesis.s3.method.m1.body: with country-pair and year fixed effects, OLS estimation.
thesis.s3.method.m1.label: Augmented gravity equation
thesis.s3.method.m2.body: Every specification clears the RESET test before being interpreted.
thesis.s3.method.m2.label: Misspecification handled.
thesis.s3.method.m3.body: swaps in alternate fixed-effect structures (country-time-varying vs. time-invariant pair) to stress-test EU and RTA coefficients.
thesis.s3.method.m3.label: Robustness table
thesis.s3.method.m4.body: GDP, distance, contiguity, common language, colonial ties, FDI stock, exchange rate, EU, common currency.
thesis.s3.method.m4.label: Standard controls retained:
thesis.s4.fallback: The full PDF is hosted by Erasmus University Rotterdam.
thesis.s4.lede: All 78 pages: abstract, literature review, methodology, results tables, robustness checks, appendices and references. The Erasmus repository hosts the canonical PDF.
thesis.sub: Institutions, infrastructure, and globalization, and how each moves bilateral aggregate exports between 36 OECD economies over the 1995-2018 panel. A traditional gravity equation extended with state fragility, an innovation index, and economic + political globalization indices.
